content:
# Disagreement Log -- Numbers Chapter 8

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

---

### 8:1 -- "saying" vs. "and said" (le'mor)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-spoke YHWH unto Moses, saying:" |
| LOGOS | "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying," |
| PNEUMA | "YHWH spoke to Moses and said:" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:" -- The Hebrew le'mor is the infinitive construct of 'amar ("to say"), functioning as a standard speech-introduction formula. LOGOS preserves "saying" faithfully. PNEUMA renders "and said," which is natural English but loses the infinitive character and the conjunction-initial pattern ("And..."). EMET preserves the conjunction but uses awkward "unto." The consensus follows LOGOS with the conjunction "And" preserved per binding convention. PNEUMA's omission of "And" at the verse opening violates the conjunction-preservation rule.

---

### 8:2 -- "When you set up the lamps" vs. "In your causing to go up the lamps" vs. "When you set up the seven lamps" (be-ha'alotekha et ha-nerot)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "In-your-causing-to-go-up the-lamps, toward the-face of the-lampstand they-shall-give-light, seven of the-lamps" |
| LOGOS | "When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light toward the front of the lampstand" |
| PNEUMA | "When you set up the seven lamps, they must cast their light forward, illuminating the area in front of the lampstand" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give their light toward the front of the lampstand" -- EMET's hyper-literal "in your causing to go up" preserves the hiphil morphology but is unreadable English. LOGOS's "set up" captures the practical sense while preserving formality. PNEUMA adds interpretive expansions ("cast their light forward, illuminating the area in front") not present in the Hebrew, and moves "seven" to modify the first clause rather than the second. The MT has "seven of the lamps" (shiv'at ha-nerot) as the subject of "shall give light," not as a modifier of the initial clause. The consensus follows LOGOS's clause structure, adds "their" before "light" for a slightly more natural English rendering (ya'iru = "they shall give light"), and uses "front" for mul penei rather than EMET's "face of."

---

### 8:2 -- "toward the front of the lampstand" vs. "toward the face of the lampstand" vs. "illuminating the area in front of the lampstand" (el mul penei ha-menorah)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "toward the-face of the-lampstand" |
| LOGOS | "toward the front of the lampstand" |
| PNEUMA | "illuminating the area in front of the lampstand" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "toward the front of the lampstand" -- The Hebrew mul penei literally means "opposite the face of." EMET preserves "face" (penei), which is the literal Hebrew but unusual in English for a lampstand. LOGOS's "front" is the standard English equivalent for this spatial term. PNEUMA restructures entirely with "illuminating the area in front of," adding words not in the Hebrew. The Vaticanus reads kata prosopon tes luchnias ("toward the face of the lampstand"), supporting the spatial-directional sense. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:3 -- "And Aaron did so" vs. "Aaron did so" (va-ya'as ken Aharon)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-did so Aaron: toward the-face of the-lampstand he-caused-to-go-up its-lamps, as-which YHWH commanded Moses" |
| LOGOS | "And Aaron did so; he set up its lamps toward the front of the lampstand, as YHWH had commanded Moses." |
| PNEUMA | "Aaron did so. He mounted the lamps so that they shone toward the front of the lampstand, exactly as YHWH had commanded Moses." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And Aaron did so; he set up its lamps toward the front of the lampstand, as YHWH had commanded Moses." -- PNEUMA omits "And" (violating binding convention) and uses "mounted" (interpretive; Hebrew he'elah = "caused to go up/set up," not specifically "mounted") and "so that they shone" (interpretive expansion of the directional clause). PNEUMA also adds "exactly" before "as YHWH had commanded," which is not in the Hebrew ka'asher. EMET's "he-caused-to-go-up" is too literal for readable English. LOGOS provides a formal, accurate rendering. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:4 -- "workmanship" vs. "work" vs. "how the lampstand was constructed" (ma'aseh ha-menorah)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-this was the-work of the-lampstand: hammered-work of gold -- unto its-base, unto its-flowers, hammered-work it was -- according-to-the-pattern which YHWH showed Moses, so he-made the-lampstand." |
| LOGOS | "And this was the workmanship of the lampstand: hammered work of gold, from its base to its flowers it was hammered work. According to the pattern which YHWH had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand." |
| PNEUMA | "This is how the lampstand was constructed: it was hammered gold from its base to its blossoms -- a single piece of beaten work. Moses had made the lampstand following the exact pattern YHWH had shown him." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And this was the workmanship of the lampstand: hammered work of gold, from its base to its flowers it was hammered work. According to the pattern that YHWH had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand." -- PNEUMA omits "And," restructures the verse completely, adds "a single piece of" (not in the Hebrew), uses "blossoms" for pirkhah (EMET/LOGOS: "flowers"), adds "exact" before "pattern," names "Moses" as maker explicitly, and changes "which" to "following." EMET uses "unto" (archaic) for ad ("to/unto"). LOGOS provides a faithful formal rendering. The consensus follows LOGOS with minor adjustment ("that" for "which" for smoother relative clause). "Flowers" is retained for pirkhah (from the root parakh, "to bud/blossom"); PNEUMA's "blossoms" is defensible but less traditional.

---

### 8:5 -- "saying" vs. "and said" (le'mor)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-spoke YHWH unto Moses, saying:" |
| LOGOS | "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying," |
| PNEUMA | "YHWH spoke to Moses and said:" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Same as v. 1. "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:" -- PNEUMA omits "And" and renders le'mor as "and said." The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:6 -- "Take the Levites from among" vs. "Separate the Levites from the rest of" (qakh et ha-Leviyyim mi-tokh)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Take the-Levites from-the-midst of the-Sons of Israel, and-you-shall-purify them." |
| LOGOS | "Take the Levites from among the Sons of Israel and cleanse them." |
| PNEUMA | "Separate the Levites from the rest of the Sons of Israel and make them ritually clean." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "Take the Levites from among the Sons of Israel and cleanse them." -- The Hebrew qakh is simply "take" (imperative of laqakh). PNEUMA renders "Separate," which is the verb of v. 14 (hivdalta) rather than v. 6. PNEUMA also adds "ritually" before "clean," an interpretive addition. EMET's "purify" and LOGOS's "cleanse" both render ve-tiharta (piel of taher); "cleanse" is slightly more natural in English. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:7 -- "water of purification" vs. "water of sin-purification" (mei khattat)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "sprinkle upon-them water of sin-purification" |
| LOGOS | "sprinkle upon them water of purification" |
| PNEUMA | "Sprinkle the water of purification on them" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "sprinkle upon them water of purification" -- The Hebrew mei khattat literally means "water of sin/sin-offering." EMET preserves both elements with "sin-purification." LOGOS and PNEUMA render the functional sense "water of purification," following the LXX's hydor hagnismou ("water of purification/sanctification"). The term khattat here refers to the purifying function of the water rather than a sin offering per se. The consensus follows LOGOS. Note that PNEUMA also reorders ("the water of purification on them" vs. "upon them water of purification"); the consensus preserves the Hebrew word order where "upon them" (alehem) precedes the object.

---

### 8:7 -- "let them pass a razor" vs. "have them shave their entire bodies with a razor" (ve-he'eviru ta'ar al kol besaram)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "and-they-shall-cause-to-pass a-razor over all their-flesh" |
| LOGOS | "and let them pass a razor over all their flesh" |
| PNEUMA | "Then have them shave their entire bodies with a razor" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "and let them pass a razor over all their flesh" -- PNEUMA restructures entirely: "their entire bodies" for "all their flesh" (kol besaram) loses the Hebrew's specific word basar ("flesh"), and "shave" replaces the distinctive Hebrew idiom "cause to pass" (he'eviru). PNEUMA also uses "Then" instead of "and," breaking the conjunction chain. LOGOS preserves the Hebrew idiom naturally. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:7 -- "and they shall be clean" vs. "and so they shall be clean" vs. "and so make themselves clean" (ve-hittahharu)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "and-they-shall-purify-themselves" |
| LOGOS | "and they shall be clean" |
| PNEUMA | "and so make themselves clean" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "and so they shall be clean" -- The Hebrew ve-hittahharu is the hitpa'el (reflexive) of taher, literally "and they shall purify/cleanse themselves." EMET preserves the reflexive; LOGOS renders result-state "shall be clean"; PNEUMA adds "and so" and preserves the reflexive agency. The consensus blends LOGOS's phrasing with PNEUMA's connective "and so," which captures the consequential sense (the three-step process results in cleanness).

---

### 8:8 -- "And they shall take" vs. "Then let them take" vs. "Then they are to take" (ve-laqkhu)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-they-shall-take a-young-bull, a-son of cattle, and-its-grain-offering: fine-flour mixed with-oil; and-a-young-bull, a-son of cattle, a-second, you-shall-take for-a-sin-offering." |
| LOGOS | "Then let them take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and a second young bull you shall take for a sin offering." |
| PNEUMA | "Then they are to take a young bull along with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil. You yourself are to take a second young bull as a purification offering." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And they shall take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and a second young bull you shall take for a sin offering." -- EMET preserves the raw Hebrew "a son of cattle" (ben baqar), which is the Hebrew idiom for "young bull" but is not normal English. LOGOS and PNEUMA both render "young bull" correctly. PNEUMA adds "along with" (interpretive expansion), "You yourself" (emphasis not in Hebrew), and renders khattat as "purification offering" rather than "sin offering." The term khattat when designating a sacrifice is traditionally rendered "sin offering" and is so rendered consistently in Leviticus 4–5; the consensus retains "sin offering." The conjunction "And" is restored (LOGOS/PNEUMA use "Then"). The consensus follows LOGOS's structure with the conjunction corrected.

---

### 8:9 -- "tent of meeting" vs. "Tent of Meeting" (Ohel Mo'ed)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "the-Tent of Meeting" |
| LOGOS | "the tent of meeting" |
| PNEUMA | "the Tent of Meeting" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "the Tent of Meeting" -- This is a proper designation for the sacred structure. EMET and PNEUMA capitalize; LOGOS lowercases. The consensus follows the majority and capitalizes "Tent of Meeting" as a proper noun throughout.

---

### 8:10 -- Minor structural differences
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-you-shall-bring-near the-Levites before YHWH, and-shall-lay the-Sons of Israel their-hands upon the-Levites." |
| LOGOS | "And you shall bring the Levites before YHWH, and the Sons of Israel shall lay their hands upon the Levites." |
| PNEUMA | "When you present the Levites before YHWH, the Sons of Israel are to place their hands on the Levites." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And you shall bring the Levites before YHWH, and the Sons of Israel shall lay their hands upon the Levites." -- PNEUMA restructures with "When you present" (temporal clause not in Hebrew; the Hebrew has two consecutive imperatives joined by vav) and uses "place" instead of "lay" for samekhu. The Hebrew samakh means "to lean/press" (hands upon), and "lay" is the traditional English rendering. PNEUMA also omits "And" at the start. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:11 -- "wave" vs. "offer" vs. "present" and "performing the service" vs. "dedicated to performing YHWH's service" (ve-henif... tenufah / la-avod et avodat YHWH)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-shall-wave Aaron the-Levites a-wave-offering before YHWH from-with the-Sons of Israel, and-they-shall-be for-doing the-service of YHWH." |
| LOGOS | "And Aaron shall offer the Levites before YHWH as a wave offering from the Sons of Israel, and they shall be for performing the service of YHWH." |
| PNEUMA | "Aaron is then to present the Levites before YHWH as a wave offering from the Sons of Israel, so that they may be dedicated to performing YHWH's service." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And Aaron shall present the Levites before YHWH as a wave offering from the Sons of Israel, and they shall be for performing the service of YHWH." -- The verb henif means "to wave/elevate" (hiphil of nuf). EMET translates literally as "wave"; LOGOS uses "offer"; PNEUMA uses "present." Since the term tenufah ("wave offering") is retained as the object, using "wave" as the verb is redundant ("wave... as a wave offering"). "Present" captures the act of bringing before YHWH while letting "wave offering" carry the ritual specificity. PNEUMA adds "so that they may be dedicated to" (not in the Hebrew; the Hebrew simply says ve-hayu la-avod, "and they shall be for serving"). PNEUMA also omits "And." The consensus uses "present" as the verb, follows LOGOS for the remainder.

---

### 8:12 -- "the young bulls" vs. "the bulls" vs. "the two bulls" (ha-parim)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "the-Levites shall-lay their-hands upon the-head of the-young-bulls; and-make the-one a-sin-offering and the-one a-burnt-offering to-YHWH, to-atone over the-Levites." |
| LOGOS | "And the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bulls; and offer the one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to YHWH, to make atonement for the Levites." |
| PNEUMA | "The Levites are to lay their hands on the heads of the two bulls. Offer one as a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering to YHWH, to make atonement for the Levites." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bulls; and offer the one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to YHWH, to make atonement for the Levites." -- EMET uses "the-head" (singular rosh) where the Hebrew has the singular distributive (each bull has one head), but LOGOS/PNEUMA use the English-natural plural "heads." PNEUMA adds "two" before "bulls" (not in the Hebrew ha-parim; the number is contextual from v. 8 but not stated here). PNEUMA again uses "purification offering" for khattat; the consensus retains "sin offering." EMET omits the conjunction "And" and uses "atone over" for le-khapper al; the consensus follows LOGOS's "make atonement for." The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:13 -- "set... before" vs. "station... before" and "wave them" vs. "present them" vs. "offer them" (ve-ha'amadta / ve-henafta)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-you-shall-stand the-Levites before Aaron and-before his-sons, and-you-shall-wave them a-wave-offering to-YHWH." |
| LOGOS | "And you shall set the Levites before Aaron and before his sons, and offer them as a wave offering to YHWH." |
| PNEUMA | "Station the Levites before Aaron and his sons and present them as a wave offering to YHWH." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And you shall set the Levites before Aaron and before his sons, and present them as a wave offering to YHWH." -- EMET's "you-shall-stand" is overly literal for he'emadta (hiphil of amad, "to cause to stand"). LOGOS's "set" and PNEUMA's "station" both work; "set" is more concise. For ve-henafta otam tenufah, "present" is used (as in v. 11) to avoid the redundancy of "wave... wave offering" and "offer... wave offering." PNEUMA omits "And" and "before" the second time; the consensus preserves both per the Hebrew.

---

### 8:14 -- "separate" vs. "set apart" (ve-hivdalta)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-you-shall-separate the-Levites from-the-midst of the-Sons of Israel, and-the-Levites shall-be mine." |
| LOGOS | "Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the Sons of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine." |
| PNEUMA | "In this way you are to set the Levites apart from the rest of the Sons of Israel, and the Levites will belong to me." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And you shall separate the Levites from among the Sons of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine." -- LOGOS uses "Thus" instead of "And" for the conjunction; the binding convention requires preserving "And." PNEUMA uses "set apart" (a phrasal verb expanding the single Hebrew verb hivdalta), adds "the rest of" (not in Hebrew mi-tokh), and renders li ("mine") as "belong to me" (interpretive expansion). The consensus follows EMET's conjunction and LOGOS's structure, restoring "And" at the opening.

---

### 8:15 -- "serve the Tent of Meeting" vs. "begin their service in" (la-avod et Ohel Mo'ed)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-after-that shall-come the-Levites to-serve the-Tent of Meeting; and-you-shall-purify them and-you-shall-wave them a-wave-offering." |
| LOGOS | "And after that the Levites shall come in to serve the tent of meeting. And you shall cleanse them and offer them as a wave offering." |
| PNEUMA | "After you have purified the Levites and presented them as a wave offering, they may begin their service in the Tent of Meeting." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And after that the Levites shall come in to serve the Tent of Meeting. And you shall cleanse them and present them as a wave offering." -- PNEUMA entirely reverses the clause order of the Hebrew, placing the purification before the service entry, and adds "begin" (not in Hebrew). The Hebrew has two sequential statements: (1) after that, the Levites shall come to serve; (2) and you shall purify and wave them. PNEUMA also omits "And." LOGOS's structure follows the Hebrew; the consensus follows LOGOS with "present" for the wave-offering verb (as in vv. 11, 13) and "Tent of Meeting" capitalized.

---

### 8:16 -- "wholly given" vs. "given, given" vs. "given unreservedly" (netunim netunim)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "For given, given they are to-me from-the-midst of the-Sons of Israel; in-place of the-opening of every womb, the-firstborn of all from-the-Sons of Israel, I-have-taken them to-me." |
| LOGOS | "For they are wholly given to me from among the Sons of Israel; in place of every one who opens the womb, the firstborn of all the Sons of Israel, I have taken them for myself." |
| PNEUMA | "For the Levites have been given to me unreservedly from among the Sons of Israel. I have taken them for myself in place of every firstborn -- the first to open every womb among the Sons of Israel." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "For they are wholly given to me from among the Sons of Israel; in place of every one that opens the womb, the firstborn of all the Sons of Israel, I have taken them for myself." -- The emphatic doubling netunim netunim is rendered: EMET preserves it literally as "given, given"; LOGOS compresses to "wholly given"; PNEUMA renders "given... unreservedly." LOGOS's "wholly given" captures the emphatic force concisely. PNEUMA restructures the verse, adds "the Levites" as explicit subject (the Hebrew uses the pronoun hemmah), splits into two sentences, and reorders the firstborn clause. EMET's "the opening of every womb" is literal for piter kol rekhem; LOGOS's "every one who opens the womb" is clearer. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:17 -- "both man and beast" vs. "among man and among beast" vs. "whether human or animal" (ba-adam u-va-behemah)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "among-man and-among-beast" |
| LOGOS | "both man and beast" |
| PNEUMA | "whether human or animal" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "both man and beast" -- EMET's "among man and among beast" preserves the prepositional construction ba-adam u-va-behemah but is awkward. LOGOS's "both man and beast" is idiomatic English and captures the inclusive scope. PNEUMA's "whether human or animal" is natural English but shifts the register and vocabulary (adam = "man/human," behemah = "beast/animal"). The consensus follows LOGOS for its balance of accuracy and readability.

---

### 8:17 -- "struck" vs. "struck down" (hakkoti)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "I-struck every firstborn in-the-land of Egypt" |
| LOGOS | "I struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt" |
| PNEUMA | "I struck down every firstborn in the land of Egypt" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "I struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt" -- PNEUMA adds "down" to intensify; the Hebrew hikketi (hiphil of nakah) simply means "I struck." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS.

---

### 8:18 -- Minor conjunction difference
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-I-took the-Levites in-place of every firstborn among-the-Sons of Israel." |
| LOGOS | "And I have taken the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Sons of Israel." |
| PNEUMA | "But I have taken the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Sons of Israel." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And I have taken the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Sons of Israel." -- PNEUMA uses "But" for the Hebrew vav conjunction, introducing an adversative sense not present in the Hebrew. The Hebrew va-eqqakh is simply "and I took." The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:19 -- "as a gift" vs. "as given-ones" vs. "assigned" (netunim)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-I-gave the-Levites as given-ones to-Aaron and-to-his-sons from-the-midst of the-Sons of Israel..." |
| LOGOS | "And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the Sons of Israel..." |
| PNEUMA | "And I have assigned the Levites to Aaron and his sons from among the Sons of Israel..." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the Sons of Israel..." -- EMET's "as given-ones" is over-literal for netunim (preserving the passive participle form). LOGOS's "as a gift" captures the sense of dedication idiomatically. PNEUMA uses "assigned," which loses the giving/gift language entirely. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:19 -- "when the Sons of Israel draw near to the sanctuary" vs. "when drawing near" vs. "should they come too close" (be-geshet benei Yisra'el el ha-qodesh)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "when-drawing-near the-Sons of Israel unto the-holy-place" |
| LOGOS | "when the Sons of Israel come near to the sanctuary" |
| PNEUMA | "should the Sons of Israel come too close to the sanctuary" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "when the Sons of Israel draw near to the sanctuary" -- PNEUMA adds "too close" (interpretive; the Hebrew simply says be-geshet, "when drawing near," without implying excess). EMET's "the-holy-place" is literal for ha-qodesh; LOGOS/PNEUMA render "sanctuary," which is the standard English for qodesh in this context. The consensus uses "draw near" (preserving the Hebrew root nagash more closely than LOGOS's "come near") and "sanctuary" (following LOGOS/PNEUMA).

---

### 8:20 -- "did to the Levites" vs. "did everything with the Levites" (va-ya'as... la-Leviyyim)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-did Moses and-Aaron and-all the-congregation of the-Sons of Israel to-the-Levites according-to-all which YHWH commanded Moses concerning-the-Levites -- so did the-Sons of Israel to-them." |
| LOGOS | "And Moses and Aaron and the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel did to the Levites according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses concerning the Levites; so the Sons of Israel did to them." |
| PNEUMA | "Moses, Aaron, and the entire community of the Sons of Israel did everything with the Levites just as YHWH had commanded Moses concerning them. That is precisely what the Sons of Israel did with them." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And Moses and Aaron and the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel did to the Levites according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses concerning the Levites; so the Sons of Israel did to them." -- PNEUMA omits "And," renders "did everything with" (Hebrew is "did to," la-Leviyyim = "to the Levites," not "with"), adds "That is precisely what" (not in Hebrew ken asu = "so they did"), and changes "the Levites" to "them" in the second clause. The Hebrew deliberately repeats "the Levites" (la-Leviyyim) twice; PNEUMA's pronoun substitution loses this emphasis. The consensus follows LOGOS.

---

### 8:21 -- "purified themselves" vs. "purified themselves from sin" (va-yitkhatte'u)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-purified-themselves the-Levites and-they-washed their-garments, and-waved Aaron them a-wave-offering before YHWH; and-atoned Aaron over-them to-purify-them." |
| LOGOS | "And the Levites purified themselves and washed their garments, and Aaron offered them as a wave offering before YHWH, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them." |
| PNEUMA | "The Levites purified themselves from sin and washed their clothes. Then Aaron presented them as a wave offering before YHWH and made atonement for them to purify them." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And the Levites purified themselves and washed their garments, and Aaron presented them as a wave offering before YHWH, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them." -- The Hebrew va-yitkhatte'u is the hitpa'el of khata', literally "they de-sinned themselves." PNEUMA makes "from sin" explicit, which is the etymological sense but goes beyond what the Hebrew text states (the verb alone suffices). PNEUMA also omits "And," splits into two sentences, uses "clothes" for begadim (EMET/LOGOS: "garments" -- the more traditional rendering), and drops the second mention of "Aaron" in the atonement clause. The consensus follows LOGOS with "present" for the wave-offering verb and retains "cleanse" for le-taharatam (PNEUMA: "purify"; both are valid, but "cleanse" has been used consistently in this chapter).

---

### 8:22 -- "perform their service" vs. "do their service" vs. structure differences
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-after-that came the-Levites to-do their-service in-the-Tent of Meeting before Aaron and-before his-sons; as-which YHWH commanded Moses concerning the-Levites, so they-did to-them." |
| LOGOS | "And after that the Levites came in to perform their service in the tent of meeting before Aaron and before his sons. As YHWH had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them." |
| PNEUMA | "After that, the Levites came to perform their service at the Tent of Meeting under the direction of Aaron and his sons. They carried out with the Levites exactly what YHWH had commanded Moses." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And after that the Levites came in to perform their service in the Tent of Meeting before Aaron and before his sons. As YHWH had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them." -- PNEUMA omits "And," renders lifnei ("before") as "under the direction of" (interpretive expansion), restructures the obedience formula ("They carried out with the Levites exactly what YHWH had commanded Moses" vs. "As YHWH had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them"), and adds "exactly." The Hebrew preserves the formulaic structure ka'asher tsivvah YHWH... ken asu lahem. The consensus follows LOGOS with "Tent of Meeting" capitalized.

---

### 8:23 -- "saying" vs. "and said" (le'mor)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-spoke YHWH unto Moses, saying:" |
| LOGOS | "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying," |
| PNEUMA | "YHWH spoke to Moses and said:" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Same as vv. 1 and 5. The consensus follows LOGOS with "And" preserved.

---

### 8:24 -- "he shall come to serve in the host of the service" vs. "enter active duty" vs. structure (yavo' li-tsevo' tsava' ba-avodat)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "from-a-son of twenty and-five years and-upward he-shall-come to-serve the-service in-the-service of the-Tent of Meeting." |
| LOGOS | "from twenty-five years old and upward they shall enter to serve in the host of the service of the tent of meeting." |
| PNEUMA | "From twenty-five years of age and older, a Levite is to enter active duty and serve in the work of the Tent of Meeting." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "from twenty-five years old and upward he shall come to serve in the host of the service of the Tent of Meeting." -- The Hebrew uses the singular yavo' ("he shall come"), not the plural. LOGOS uses "they shall enter" (plural); PNEUMA adds "a Levite" as explicit subject and renders the cognate accusative li-tsevo' tsava' as "active duty" (a dynamic gloss that captures the military metaphor but loses the Hebrew's formal structure). EMET preserves "a son of twenty and five years" (the Hebrew idiom mi-ben khamesh ve-esrim shanah), which is over-literal. The consensus uses the singular "he shall come" (following the MT), renders the cognate construction "to serve in the host of the service" (following LOGOS), and capitalizes "Tent of Meeting."

---

### 8:25 -- "withdraw from the host of the service" vs. "retire from the active service force" (yashuv mi-tseva' ha-avodah)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-from-a-son of fifty years he-shall-return from-the-host of the-service, and-he-shall-not serve again." |
| LOGOS | "And from the age of fifty years they shall withdraw from the host of the service and shall serve no more." |
| PNEUMA | "But at the age of fifty, he must retire from the active service force and serve no longer in that capacity." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And from the age of fifty years he shall withdraw from the host of the service, and shall serve no more." -- PNEUMA uses "But" (adversative not in Hebrew), "retire" (modern term; the Hebrew yashuv simply means "he shall return/turn back"), adds "in that capacity" (not in Hebrew), and omits "And." LOGOS uses plural "they" where the Hebrew is singular. EMET preserves the singular correctly. The consensus uses the singular "he" and LOGOS's "withdraw" (a formal equivalent for yashuv in this context), preserving "And" at the start.

---

### 8:26 -- "minister with his brothers" vs. "assist his fellow Levites" and "keep the charge" vs. "performing guard duties" (ve-sheret et ekhav / lishmor mishmeret)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And-he-shall-minister with his-brothers in-the-Tent of Meeting to-keep a-charge, and-service he-shall-not serve. Thus you-shall-do to-the-Levites concerning-their-charges." |
| LOGOS | "They may minister with their brothers in the tent of meeting, to keep the charge, but they shall do no service. Thus you shall do to the Levites concerning their duties." |
| PNEUMA | "He may assist his fellow Levites in the Tent of Meeting by performing guard duties, but he is not to do the regular service work. This is how you are to arrange the duties of the Levites." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "He may minister with his brothers in the Tent of Meeting to keep the charge, but he shall do no service. Thus you shall do to the Levites concerning their charges." -- The Hebrew ve-sheret means "and he shall minister" (piel of sharat); PNEUMA's "assist" is too informal and loses the technical priestly vocabulary. "His brothers" (ekhav) is rendered by PNEUMA as "his fellow Levites" (interpretive expansion). LOGOS shifts to plural "They... their brothers" where the Hebrew is singular throughout. PNEUMA's "performing guard duties" for lishmor mishmeret adds "performing" and "duties" not in the text; "to keep the charge" is the traditional rendering of the cognate construction. PNEUMA's "This is how you are to arrange" renders kakhah ta'aseh ("thus you shall do") with unnecessary paraphrase. The consensus uses the Hebrew's singular voice, EMET's "minister with his brothers," LOGOS's "keep the charge," and "Thus you shall do" for the closing formula. "Charges" (mishmerotam) is preferred over LOGOS's "duties" as it preserves the semantic connection to "charge" (mishmeret) earlier in the verse.
